Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: born 03/1984, Eastridge County, employer “Pineglen Community Clinic”, role “billing clerk”, using a home tablet model ZT-550 with MAC fragment ab:cd:**:**:90, and referencing account nickname “HSA-Spring” with suffix 7712

must_detect.matched_text:
Scope includes customer, member, employee, and analyst interactions; our policy prohibits selective disclosure of material non-public information to sell-side or buy-side analysts, and IR releases are limited to previously announced metrics and current report filings (CR-8 equivalents).
Benign example: the Q1 clinical network expansion and fee schedule were announced on 03/15/2025 in a public CR-8 statement and webcast; references to those facts in meetings are not MNPI.
Data minimization: only collect what is reasonably necessary; generic demographics (e.g., 30–39 age range, state-level location, generic job category) are treated as non-identifying unless combined with other data.
Form labels may appear in systems (Name:, SSN:, MRN:, Account No:, Device ID:), but placeholders such as 000-00-0000, MRN 9999999, or account XXXX-XXXX-1234 are non-actionable and should be ignored in DSAR fulfillment.
Role mailboxes (privacy@northwaymeridian.example, ir@northwaymeridian.example) and the public helpline listed on our website are the only intake channels for consumer requests and analyst inquiries.
Retention: customer service call recordings kept 2 years; clinical device telemetry event logs 18 months; card-issuing records 7 years; delete or de-identify earlier if the purpose is satisfied and no legal hold is in place.
Cross-border transfers (e.g., to processors at Cumulonix Data Processing LLC in Arcadia State and to Northway Meridian Services Ltd in Northlake Province) use SCC-equivalent clauses and are mapped in our vendor inventory; no government IDs are transferred.
Access control: employees in Care Operations (roles: intake specialist, nurse navigator) and Retail Banking (roles: teller, branch supervisor) receive least-privilege access; analysts receive only public packs.
Intentional positive cluster (incident review): Case NBH-IR-0418 logged a caller described as born 03/1984, Eastridge County, employer “Pineglen Community Clinic”, role “billing clerk”, using a home tablet model ZT-550 with MAC fragment ab:cd:**:**:90, and referencing account nickname “HSA-Spring” with suffix 7712 during a grievance intake; these combined quasi-identifiers require redaction under DSAR and litigation hold.
Outside that context, separated weak identifiers such as a masked ZIP (000**), a month-year of birth, or a job title alone must not be treated as reasonably linkable without additional data.
Analyst interactions: prebrief materials are scrubbed for MNPI and reviewed by Legal and IR; if a question seeks non-public metrics, staff must redirect to a public CR-8 update or decline to comment.
Financial aid desk scripts include invalid identifier-shaped examples (e.g., CUSIP-like 0000000Z, routing 000000000) solely for training; these are not real securities or banking identifiers.
Consumer rights: California residents may submit access, correction, and deletion requests; we verify using two data points (e.g., device type + last four of a non-financial member number) and never request SSN for verification.
Healthcare privacy: when acting as a HIPAA covered entity, we segregate PHI; when acting as a GLBA financial institution, we apply the privacy rule and safeguard rule; opt-out notices for financial marketing are provided annually.
Data subject access responses exclude privileged materials, trade secrets, and any MNPI; we provide a high-level description when exact identifiers are masked or aggregated.
